title: ophrys bilunulata subsp. caesiella
intro: Ophrys bilunulata subsp. caesiella, also known as the caesiella bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid characterized by its unique, bee-like appearance, which attracts pollinators. The caesiella bee orchid has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic content. Herbal preparations of the caesiella bee orchid can be used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems, and it is often consumed as a tea or used in topical applications.
